title: Pediatric Anemia CDSS Rule
description: EP-4 (FR-WP4-01) adds 9 rule-governance fields, all REQUIRED as keys under this schema's `additionalProperties: false` — there is no incremental rollout path under that constraint, so this was landed as one reviewable diff. Several are legitimately nullable/empty (`retireDate`, `clinicalApprovers`, `requiredTestCaseIds`, `sourcePassageId`): absence of the underlying fact is expressed with an explicit typed null or empty array, never by omitting the key, so a rule that forgot the field entirely still fails validation. `sourcePassageId: null` is schema-legal (D-EP3-3, Risk 6) for a newly authored rule not yet backfilled; `scripts/validate-kb.mjs` is the stricter policy layer that rejects null for the shipped KB (EP4-T2 flips `REQUIRE_RULE_SOURCE_PASSAGE_ID` once the codemod lands). Structural validity here never implies clinical validity, safety, or that a named human clinician reviewed anything — see `clinicalApprovers`.

version.description: Semver-shaped governance version for this rule record (not the KB or app version). REQUIRED, non-null: every governed rule has a version, starting at "1.0.0" for the EP-4 backfill (FR-WP4-02).

effectiveDate.description: Date this rule's current content became effective. REQUIRED, non-null. For the EP-4 backfill this is uniformly the KB's `reviewedThrough` date (modules/anemia/evidence.json) — a governance-record stamp, not a per-rule clinical review date.

retireDate.description: Date this rule was retired/superseded, or `null` while still active. REQUIRED key, legitimately nullable — `null` is the normal value for every currently active rule, never an omission.

owner.description: The role/team accountable for this rule's content — REQUIRED, non-null, and deliberately NOT a person's name (this is an unvalidated research prototype with no credentialed clinical owner; the `role:`/`team:` prefix is schema-enforced so a named human cannot be substituted by mistake). See `changeRationale` for why no clinical re-review occurred.

safetyClass.description: REQUIRED, non-null, closed enum (not free text). `safety-critical` = alert-output/safety-category rules (emergency/urgent/important flags and missing-data-that-blocks-safe-use prompts); `diagnostic` = differential-category candidate-ranking rules; `informational` = interpretive-note and adaptive-question rules that inform but do not themselves flag a hazard or rank a pattern. Derived deterministically from the rule's existing `category` field — see scripts/evidence/backfill-rule-governance.mjs header for the exact derivation table.

requiredTestCaseIds.description: REQUIRED key, legitimately empty (`[]` = no test-case linkage yet — never "exempt from testing"). Populated mechanically with the relative fixture path(s) under tests/witness/ or tests/fixtures/ that make this rule fire (scripts/rule-coverage.mjs's activation-witness mapping), not hand-curated.

changeRationale.description: REQUIRED, non-null, honest statement of why this rule's governance record last changed. For the EP-4 backfill this is a uniform string naming the mechanical governance backfill and explicitly disclaiming any clinical re-review — never an invented per-rule clinical rationale.

sourcePassageId.description: REQUIRED key. Nullable in this schema (D-EP3-3, Risk 6 — legitimate absence for a not-yet-backfilled rule), though `scripts/validate-kb.mjs` rejects null for the shipped KB once EP4-T2 lands. Names exactly one passage record in modules/anemia/evidence.json#sources[].passages[] (D-EP3-1: rule.evidence[] keeps its existing source-level shape; this field is the passage-level pointer). A present value must resolve to a real passage id; per D-EP3-6, it is bound to a `source-supported` passage only when unambiguous, and otherwise falls back to that source's `<SOURCE_ID>#implementation-proposal` sentinel — both are legal values here, the distinction is enforced by src/evidence.js#isBindableAsSourceSupported and scripts/validate-kb.mjs, not by this schema.

clinicalApprovers.description: REQUIRED key, and under this schema version it MUST be exactly `[]` — `maxItems: 0` makes a populated approver list a hard schema violation, not merely a test failure (D-4/AC-D4; hardened per reviewer-gate finding 4, 2026-07-21). `[]` means NO credentialed clinical approval yet. No credentialed human clinician has approved any rule in this knowledge base, so there is currently no legal non-empty value, and ARC/council-review/rf output is explicitly NOT a valid source for one. Raising `maxItems` is the deliberate, reviewable act by which this project would first claim clinical sign-off; it must never be done to make a build pass. `items` is retained so that when a real attestation process exists, the element shape is already specified.

op.description: EP-1 / SPIKE-003 adds is-present, is-absent, is-unknown, and is-not-assessed; is-unknown and is-not-assessed are synonyms.
